name: Spec Aligner
rationale: The compiler evaluates a candidate skill but does not establish a specification-to-release chain.

name: PR Narrator
rationale: A review artifact is not a proven PR narrative and release provenance pipeline.

name: Review Router
rationale: The router proposal explicitly covers recommendation-only routing and progressive disclosure.

name: Decision Log
rationale: A redacted feedback loop does not provide a stable decision-to-release traceability model.

name: Issue Gardener
rationale: The compiler gate lacks lifecycle IDs and orphan detection for issue evidence.

name: Design Sync
rationale: A privacy-gated demonstration outline is not a browser or visual QA contract.

name: Security Buddy
rationale: The compiler proposal explicitly requires permission, threat, and data-flow evidence.

name: Knowledge Base
rationale: Source-to-skill provenance is related but does not establish a general knowledge-base runtime or retrieval contract.

name: Onboarding Map
rationale: A text decision diagram does not by itself cover onboarding context or newcomer validation.

name: Retrospective Bot
rationale: The feedback-loop proposal does not retain a release-linked retrospective evidence chain.

name: Agent Swarm
rationale: Control-plane separation intentionally excludes agent execution and does not endorse an agent swarm.

name: Playwright Scout
rationale: The demonstration boundary does not implement browser QA, routes, or visual assertions.

name: Terminal Sense
rationale: A capability contract describes evidence requirements but does not observe or operate a terminal.

name: CI Fixer
rationale: A contract harness does not own scheduling, retry ceilings, or circuit-breaker policy.

name: Release Notes
rationale: The compiler creates review evidence but not a validated release-note provenance chain.

name: Data Cleaner
rationale: A future data action needs scheduler budgets, cancellation, and side-effect containment beyond an acceptance contract.

name: Screenshot QA
rationale: The privacy gate does not define screenshot baselines or visual acceptance criteria.

name: Changelog Miner
rationale: Feedback signals are not a release-linked changelog source model.

name: Dependency Guard
rationale: The contract harness does not schedule dependency checks or own their retry and budget limits.

name: Nightly Runner
unique_gap: Allowlist budget cancellation and circuit breaker
rationale: No registered experiment owns a scheduler safety controller.

name: Context Pack
rationale: A bounded project map is related but does not define reusable context-pack selection or expiry.

name: Repo Cartographer
rationale: The proposal explicitly defines a bounded project map with evidence links and exclusions.

name: Test Pilot
rationale: The proposal explicitly requires real-output acceptance evidence beyond a zero exit code.

name: Debug Radar
rationale: An output harness helps assess results but does not define a debugging workflow.

name: Refactor Lens
rationale: Regression evidence is related but the proposal does not specify refactor analysis or decision criteria.

name: API Stitcher
rationale: A generic contract does not define API integration design or compatibility boundaries.

name: Migration Buddy
rationale: Acceptance checks are related but do not define migration sequencing or compatibility decisions.

name: Docs Whisperer
rationale: The compiler gate does not provide stale-document or release provenance handling.

name: Prompt Harness
rationale: Prompt claims fit the proposal's baseline and real-output evidence contract.

name: Ship Checklist
rationale: Acceptance evidence is related but a release checklist needs a distinct readiness and disposition model.

name: Planning with Files
rationale: Source-to-skill provenance does not define a persistent planning-file lifecycle.

name: Superpowers
rationale: The router proposal covers recommendation-only selection and progressive disclosure; source claims remain unverified.

name: Code Review
rationale: Control-plane role separation does not create a code-review implementation or approval tier.

name: Webapp Testing
rationale: A demonstration boundary is not a browser testing implementation.

name: Code Simplifier
rationale: The contract harness can verify results but does not define simplification quality criteria.

name: UI UX Pro Max
unique_gap: Verified source and safe design boundary
rationale: No reliable source identity or design scope is established.

name: MCP Builder
rationale: MCP registration and external access require an independent high-risk approval boundary.

name: Ralph Loop
unique_gap: Verified source and bounded loop semantics
rationale: Only a pause-and-handoff concept is relevant; the source is ambiguous and no loop runtime is authorized.

name: PPTX
rationale: Planning and render separation does not provide a presentation-generation runtime.

name: Skill Creator
rationale: Routing and progressive disclosure do not create or modify a Skill.
